terms.definition: The numeric ColumnTransformer or Pipeline path that applies a train-fitted scaler to continuous engineered features before a distance- or penalty-sensitive estimator.
Whether a model family uses feature magnitude (distance, gradients, penalties) or only order/thresholds (trees), determining if a scaler belongs in the FE pipeline.
Rescaling numeric columns so magnitudes and spreads are comparable across features before modeling, without changing tree split order for monotone transforms.
sklearn scaler that standardizes each feature with z = (x − μ) / σ using training mean μ and sample standard deviation σ (ddof=1). Canonical definition in scaling and normalization.
sklearn scaler that maps each feature linearly from training min–max into a target interval, default [0, 1]. Canonical definition in scaling and normalization.
Production inference applies the same fitted scaler parameters learned on training engineered features, serialized inside the deployed Pipeline.
